body_txt: When you have a misbehaving person in your care and sending them to bed without dinner is too harsh (or you just need them to not starve), an alternative punishment is giving them something bland to eat, like oatmeal or plain bread, or a food they just don't like . It will fill them up, but they won't enjoy the meal and will have to sit there and think about what they did while forcing down every unappetizing bite. The punishment becomes even more effective if the miscreant has to eat it while watching everyone else eat something delicious . This tactic is sometimes used as a way to punish children, and is combined with the "...while watching everyone else eat delicious food" variation. To really rub it in, the other children may taunt the kid who misbehaved and is (for example) stuck eating oatmeal while the others get pizza. Another common use is for prisoners to be "kept on bread and water". The threat of denying more palatable meals may be a way to control the behavior of those on the inside. Or a society might just believe that good food shouldn't be wasted on common criminals. A person who reacts badly to this may have a Lost Food Grievance . Related: This trope is not about being denied food as part of a diet. For those, compare: Contrast:
